Quick review (will contain spoilers, so don't press read more if you haven't seen it):






1. What was Martin Freeman's character? I think they were going for an outsider's reaction to Wakanda originally, but his character never gave a reaction. I know they used him for some stuff in the film, but he felt... a bit like a sore thumb.
2. The tribal stuff does not blend well with the technological stuff. Would an intellectual society use combat as election method? They could've sold it better by working more with the movies spiritual side, selling the rituals as a necessity for the powers of the panther herb or whatever. That would explain why they have build a technological society around the traditions rather than replacing the traditions like most societies do.
3. The camera movement was faulty during the CGI battles, making the battle more CGI-looking (especially towards the panther vs panther battle). More realistic camera movement would've sold the effects more.
4. Some of the designs were weird, but some were very cool.
5. I liked that they tried to give the "bad guy" some character. They did it far more than most super hero movies who completely neglect it, but personally I think they could've done more. That would've not been very mainstream by them though.
6. This is also a thing basically all of these superhero movies do wrong, and it has to do with a lot of things, partially that American actors often can not fight for real, but I like the way many Asian movies shoot fighting scenes rather than the mess that is marvel and DC superhero fights. 
7. Africa is a cool location for a film. More films should be shot there.
8. I like drums as a soundtrack. Liked it during this film as well. B^)

Overall rating as a movie: 6/10
Rating as a superhero film: 8/10
Conviction: Entertaining to watch with a friend.

Sorry to say, but this movie was painfully average. 

Now, before you grab your torches and pitchforks and call me a goddamn racist, I’ll say this: in no way was this movie bad, but in no way was it a good movie. The CGI fight scenes, the green screens, and the rhinos that showed up at one point were so blatantly obvious, it actually looked a lot like a video game. And the plot just follows the same formula as every other cookie-cutter superhero movie. I also found it to be super boring.

There were some things I liked about it, like the whole “being a king” dilemma (which was sadly underused), the score was pretty good, the cinematography was good, and you can see that they at least tried to make the villain different from other villains by giving him his own side of the story (but they threw that away as well). So it isn’t a bad movie; I’m sure a lot of people enjoyed it, but for me, the pros do not outweigh the cons.

5/10 for me.
